---
title: "Please help me solve my Samba/SMBFS woes!!"
date: 2007-03-30
forum: General Help
---

### Post by herbster on 2007-03-30
Okay, I've been having some wierdness going on for some time now with samba/smbfs.

I have 2 XP networked computers, each with a share that I'd like to mount, so I did, and here's what I have in my fstab:

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hdb1 :
UUID=e739b8ab-54a3-444f-9370-8e4a5dc8a1e5 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hdb5 :
UUID=332a0310-d839-48c1-8317-c204a64306cc none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/hdd /media/cdrom1 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/BGPC\040VISTA ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 0
//192.168.1.102/jaycdrive /media/jaycdrive smbfs credentials=/root/.smbcredentials,dmask=777,fmask=777 0 0
//192.168.1.100/bgpc2 /media/bgpc2 smbfs credentials=/root/.smbcredentialsmom,dmask=777,fmask=777 0 0
```

So my network shares are bgpc2 and jaycdrive. I have all mounted drives/shares showing in the Disk Mounter on my top panel, plus on the desktop of course.

Now, when I boot up my PC it gets strange. *Sometimes*, there will be a delay after the desktop shows and it seems to be trying to mount the shares, but they won't show up on the desktop, yet they WILL show up on the panel and are fully accessible. The only way I have gotten them to show on the desktop in this case is to sudo umount -a and sudo mount -a again.

Then, sometimes after boot up, they won't mount at all. I will click on one of the shares from the panel and it will give me an error after maybe 20-30 seconds that it couldn't be viewed. In this scenario, if I try to sudo umount -a, I get this:

```
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$ sudo umount -a
umount: /dev: device is busy
umount: /proc/bus/usb: device is busy
umount: /var/run: device is busy
umount: /sys: device is busy
umount: /: device is busy
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$ sudo mount -a
WARNING: Deficient FUSE kernel module detected. Some driver features are
         not available (swap file on NTFS, boot from NTFS by LILO), and
         unmount is not safe unless it's made sure the ntfs-3g process
         naturally terminates after calling 'umount'. The safe FUSE kernel
         driver is included in the official Linux kernels since version
         2.6.20-rc1, or in the FUSE 2.6.0 or later software packages,
         except the faulty FUSE version 2.6.2. For more help, please
         have a look at /usr/share/doc/ntfs-3g/README.Debian. Thanks

Could not resolve mount point /media/jaycdrive
Could not resolve mount point /media/bgpc2
bobby@bobby-ubuntu:~$
```

My 2 networked XP PC's are on all the time pretty much, so it's not an issue of them being turned off and so it cannot connect to them. And also, in the last week or more, when I go into Windows Network in Network Servers, I can see nothing, whereas MSHOME previously was available.

To be clear, I just want to have my shares mount on boot up and have full read/write access to them. Someone please help fix this! :mad:

Don't know If there are any more settings/output I failed to include in diagnosing the problem...

---

### Post by DugieHowsa on 2007-06-27
You should try using cifs.  This post states that smbfs is unstable and no longer under active development:

[https://bugzilla.samba.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1920](https://bugzilla.samba.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1920)

Summary: "smbfs is unmaintained and known to be severely broken." That was posted back in 2004, so needless to say probably not much has happened with smbfs in the last 3 years.

The following is also a very good post on how to configure cifs and gives a few examples on how to automatically mount cifs shares at boot.

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=288534](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=288534)

---
